tweets.text: Our ocean connects us all. Together we must advance global action to address threats to our ocean and planet. With our allies and partners, we can tackle the climate crisis, combat marine plastic pollution, support sustainable fishing, and conserve the ocean.

We are collaborating with @usaid and @cldp_doc to accelerate transformative economic growth across Central Asia by expanding trade routes, bolstering the private sector, and investing in people through training and education. https://t.co/FoNyXdygP6 https://t.co/MTF7GJUeQs

The C5+1 enhances cooperation with, and among, Central Asian countries to advance our shared goal: an independent, prosperous, and secure Central Asia that addresses common concerns in partnership with the United States. https://t.co/taPMjiiA5S https://t.co/SXdOwi8nyY

The U.S. is committed to a #PandemicAccord which will vastly improve the global health architecture for preventing, preparing for, and responding to future pandemic emergencies. Read more from our opening statement at the World Health Organization meeting: https://t.co/XYaLrr0OPd

Acting Deputy Secretary John Bass met with @UNICEF’s Omar Abdi to discuss our shared interest in providing life-saving assistance. Children and youth are among the most vulnerable when disaster strikes, underscoring the importance of UNICEF’s efforts in Türkiye and Syria. https://t.co/2ge7FgyI5U

Acting Deputy Secretary for Management and Resources John Bass met with UN Under-Secretary-General Catherine Pollard today. The U.S. is committed to ensuring a transparent, accountable UN capable of addressing the most pressing transnational challenges of our time. https://t.co/4DEPGtWcON

Together, the U.S. and Kazakhstan are combating transnational threats, corruption, and cybercrime, improving the investment and trade environment, and supporting Kazakhstan’s efforts to increase its production of low-cost clean energy. https://t.co/c5dpn9cLhm https://t.co/1SU7qIBMH4

As the world becomes more interconnected, so does U.S. foreign and domestic policy. In our latest DipNote, see how we are harnessing the talents of @ECAatState @ExchangeAlumni to build coalitions in cities across the United States. https://t.co/9qDrOY3LDG
Live now! @statedeptspox briefs reporters from the State Department, also streaming on https://t.co/8JFcDOtMHz. https://t.co/4aeevYBChP

.@SecBlinken in a conversation with @chefjoseandres on why U.S. contributions to global humanitarian aid efforts is important: “It’s the right thing to do, it’s the human thing to do, but it’s also the smart thing to do.” Listen to the full conversation: https://t.co/LFQ4Wa2uAO. https://t.co/OD6ljyxma9

More than 150 U.S. disaster response experts, including engineers and search and rescue specialists, deployed to Türkiye to help find people in the rubble after devastating earthquakes Feb. 6. Find out how they made a difference: https://t.co/JjhEc3gPhe

The U.S. has pledged $185 million to assist the people of Türkiye and Syria after devastating earthquakes. Learn how the U.S. is supporting Türkiye's response and delivering aid through humanitarian partners in Syria. https://t.co/vU5VMS1rmV

.@USAIDSavesLives works with two elite search and rescue squads from @ffxfirerescue and @lacofdpio. They deploy within hours when countries request help extricating survivors of earthquakes, like the one that struck Feb. 6. https://t.co/TAMMISUfep

.@SecBlinken with @chefjoseandres on global food security: We have seen Russia weaponize food & in ways that are not only harming people in Ukraine, but harming people around the world. Particularly countries in the global south. See the full conversation: https://t.co/FhR91UEB6o https://t.co/60bgVblmzM

One year later, Kyiv stands. Ukraine stands. Democracy stands. America — and the world — stands with Ukraine. During his trip to Kyiv @POTUS reaffirmed our unwavering and unflagging commitment to Ukraine’s democracy, sovereignty, and territorial integrity. https://t.co/6x13IVoLCr

One year after Russia’s full-scale invasion of Ukraine, 141 countries came together and voted for a comprehensive, just, and lasting peace in Ukraine — one that is in line with the @UN Charter’s most fundamental principles of sovereignty and territorial integrity. https://t.co/uQl88TgZ3s

Russia has separated thousands of Ukrainian children from their families or caregivers and held them in camps & facilities where they are exposed to a Russia-centric curriculum, according to a new report from @HRL_YaleSPH. Many children are still missing. https://t.co/nS7r9cnQ7G

NATO protects 1 billion+ people in Europe & North America from 21st-century threats, from cybercrime to pandemics such as COVID-19, and empowers all members to have an equal voice in decisions affecting the alliance. Read some examples of @NATO in action. https://t.co/Z7zNuSVBRv

.@SecBlinken with @chefjoseandres on the #DiplomaticCulinaryPartnership: Having enlisted some extraordinary chefs, I’m incredibly excited about what we can do to even better connect with other countries through food. Listen to the full conversation: https://t.co/LFQ4Wa2uAO. https://t.co/jaB9ZAvUwW

In the “Week at State,” @POTUS travels to Ukraine & Poland, @SecBlinken travels to Türkiye, Greece, and addresses the @UN on Ukraine, and a determination that Russia’s forces & officials have committed crimes against humanity. For the latest, visit https://t.co/58Uc9lIOt7. https://t.co/aHZKwEU01u

City and state diplomacy supports U.S. national security by integrating local ideas into foreign policy and fostering connections among cities, municipalities, and communities in the U.S. and abroad. Learn more about @SubnationalDip: https://t.co/mgLSWX75Jj

The United States is proud to support Angola, Liberia, Mali, and Sierra Leone with 2,054,070 COVID-19 vaccine doses delivered this week. We are working together with African partners and #COVAX to deliver safe and reliable vaccines in Africa and around the world. https://t.co/xAsyTVz0Yf

.@SecBlinken: @PEPFAR shows what we can do at our best: work together across divides, with our partners across the globe, to make progress against a seemingly intractable problem. And preserve millions of lives. Let us learn from PEPFAR and build on its lifesaving work. https://t.co/y8dmNuYzeA

.@POTUS: A dictator bent on rebuilding an empire will never be able to erase the people’s love of liberty. Brutality will never grind down the will of the free. And Ukraine — Ukraine will never be a victory for Russia. Never. https://t.co/NEf1tnpfCe

Today at the @UN, @SecBlinken shared the story of Veronika, a 10-year-old Ukrainian child who still hopes for a brighter world. We have the power and the responsibility to create that world, today and for generations to come. We cannot – will not – let one country destroy it. https://t.co/CNasir9pOF
On Ukraine, @POTUS underscored that freedom is at stake, not just for Ukrainians, but for all of us, and our children, and grandchildren. https://t.co/Rf77zGAsPr

.@POTUS: President Putin chose this war.  Every day the war continues is his choice.  He could end the war with a word. That’s why, together, we’re making sure Ukraine can defend itself. https://t.co/3ZRUYogsRJ

The U.S. is committed to supporting the people of Ukraine. In one year, the U.S. has provided $1.9 billion for refugees; violence prevention; and food, water, and hygiene. Ukraine stands strong, and the United States stands #UnitedWithUkraine. https://t.co/upGU3vPHkB

Live now! @SecBlinken participates in a UN Security Council ministerial meeting on “Maintenance of peace and security of Ukraine” in New York City. Watch the full meeting on UNTV at: https://t.co/NQZcRiPHWk https://t.co/kMi4t8pH51

Live now! @DeputySecState delivers remarks at a U.S. President’s Emergency Plan for AIDS Relief (PEPFAR) at 20 Event at the United States Institute of Peace in Washington, DC. Also streaming on YouTube at: https://t.co/p8zY5PxuMC https://t.co/d7CXKUid67
One year after President Putin's unjust invasion into their sovereign country, Ukrainians remain unbowed. #UnitedWithUkraine  https://t.co/00ejSlt7LP https://t.co/mqZru2We0w

Courage. Sacrifice. Dignity. Resolve. Since February 24, 2022 – the day President Putin unleashed his unprovoked assault – Ukrainians have embodied those words. To people the world over, the colors of the Ukrainian flag symbolize brave resistance.  
https://t.co/00ejSlt7LP https://t.co/nkGfRSI4P8

The story of Ukraine before and after President Putin’s illegal invasion is best understood through images. In this digital narrative of before and after February 24, 2022, Ukrainians’ determination is clear – and their resolve is indisputable. https://t.co/00ejSlszWh https://t.co/ZlWAoAEtYJ

The United States has not acted alone in its support for Ukraine. Since first exposing Russia’s plans to launch this invasion, we worked with the global community to support Ukraine’s resilience and determination. Read more about our support for Ukraine: https://t.co/SA3LK4vuwa
If you missed @SecBlinken's conversation on “Russia’s War on Ukraine: One Year Later” with @TheAtlantic today, there is still time to watch: https://t.co/fTx2nc5dIj
.@StateDeptSpox on the Burma military regime’s arrest and detention of prominent ethnic Kachin Christian leader, Reverend Dr. Hkalam Samson: https://t.co/6SDc6aqFdH
Live now! @StateDeptSpox briefs reporters from the State Department, also streaming on https://t.co/ETIBHiTtJI. https://t.co/KLQqvnmcji

.@POTUS on President Putin’s war of aggression and miscalculations in Ukraine: He thought @NATO would fracture and divide. Instead, NATO is more united and more unified than ever. #UnitedWithUkraine #StrongerTogether https://t.co/zIAF1j4QqQ

At 11 a.m. EST join @SecBlinken as he participates in a virtual conversation on “Russia’s War on Ukraine: One Year Later” with @TheAtlantic. Watch here:
https://t.co/lRweL9wISd
.@VP: When future generations look back at this moment, they will see that we understood the task before us and rose to the occasion.
.@SecBlinken extended warm wishes to all celebrating Losar throughout the world: May the year of the Water Hare bring you peace, prosperity, and longevity. https://t.co/xVx0P2abbj
.@SecBlinken in Türkiye: The United States is here to support you in your time of need, and we will be by your side for as long as it takes to recover and to rebuild. https://t.co/SzuP8BUcdq

.@POTUS: One year into this war, Putin no longer doubts the strength of our coalition. But he still doubts our conviction. He doubts our staying power. He doubts our continued support for Ukraine. He doubts whether @nato can remain unified. But there should be no doubt. https://t.co/IQZHjN9LxG

.@POTUS: I have just come from a visit to Kyiv, and I can report: Kyiv stands strong. Kyiv stands proud.  It stands tall.  And most important, it stands free. https://t.co/DX854sJRoX https://t.co/vPsbd60ZvR

.@VP: Together, we have provided historic assistance to Ukraine. Together, we have dealt Russia a strategic failure. Together, we have imposed unprecedented cost on Russia. And together, we have pursued energy security and reinvested in our collective defense. https://t.co/iCQOeeQ5D8

.@StateDeptSpox reviews @SecBlinken’s recent travel to Germany, Türkiye, and Greece, including meetings at the Munich Security Conference, witnessing firsthand earthquake devastation in the Hatay region of Türkiye, and participating in the U.S.-Greece Strategic Dialogue. https://t.co/Y2f16uLVtK

Over the last week, the U.S. donated more than 300,000 COVID-19 vaccine doses to Honduras in partnership with COVAX. We've now donated nearly 6.5 million vaccine doses to the people of 🇭🇳, helping to protect communities across the country from the harmful impacts of the pandemic. https://t.co/geE4jEJRvb
.@POTUS in Ukraine: One year later, Kyiv stands and Ukraine stands. Democracy stands. #UnitedWithUkraine https://t.co/xCisTERbBX

.@StateDept_GCJ on @SecBlinken’s determination that members of Russia’s forces and other Russian officials have committed crimes against humanity against the Ukrainian civilian population and holding those responsible to account: https://t.co/Pg0TdS9ouJ
Live now! @StateDeptSpox briefs reporters from the State Department, also streaming on https://t.co/JcVGiiq8Pl. https://t.co/GQkIBq8BM5

.@SecBlinken: The U.S. stands with the Nigerian people as they prepare to make their voices heard by voting in general elections on February 25. We do not support any individual candidate for office, but we strongly support free, fair, and peaceful elections. #NigeriaDecides https://t.co/zRTJSQyyl7

.@POTUS: The bravery, resilience, and determination of the Ukrainian people continues to inspire the world. And the United States, together with our allies and partners, will not waver in our commitment to the Ukrainian people as they fight for their freedom. https://t.co/0f9m54FMOs

When President Putin launched his unprovoked assault on the independent nation of Ukraine, he misjudged the character and strength of the Ukrainian people. This is what resistance and resilience looks like. #UnitedWithUkraine https://t.co/uCRQKNbGSx
Our commitment to friendship between the people of Türkiye and the people of the United States endures, and it will continue to grow and flourish for generations to come. https://t.co/VsWuQjF4hI

.@VP: The American people, you see, are in awe of the resolve of the people of Ukraine, in awe of their resilience and righteousness, their willingness to fight for freedom and liberty, and the extraordinary tenacity and leadership of President Zelenskyy. https://t.co/qpqAKHwXv4
The State Department is working through @UN agencies and NGOs to provide emergency assistance in Türkiye and in Syria. We are committed to supporting the peoples of Syria and Türkiye.

Educational and cultural cooperation between the U.S. and Greece is rich and expanding. Our partnerships on the historic #PharosSummit, workshops on protecting Greek cultural heritage from climate change and trafficking, and film sector training exemplify this collaboration. https://t.co/w99sC8YIqf

The U.S. and Europe are committed to Ukraine’s fight for freedom, providing safety for refugees, supplying military equipment, and enacting sanctions. With our continued support, not only will Ukraine prevail — freedom will prevail. https://t.co/jjtsWAKLAM

Americans have generous hearts, and in towns and cities across the country we are joining together and stepping up to help with earthquake relief efforts. We stand with the people of Türkiye and Syria. https://t.co/4NHmcY1WSE

During his visit to Türkiye, @SecBlinken witnessed the devastation and announced another $100 million to assist those in need. He underscored that we are with the peoples of Türkiye and Syria until we get the job done. https://t.co/6M1ki8raov

Together, the United States and Greece are advancing our shared goals, united by democratic values, for peace and prosperity in the Eastern Mediterranean, Western Balkans, Black Sea region, and beyond. https://t.co/VoQtLaKXL7 https://t.co/DWiqUNGRgM

On February 24, 2022, President Putin launched an unprovoked assault on an independent nation. As we approach the one-year mark of that day, one thing remains true — Ukraine stands tall, undefeated and unbowed. #UnitedWithUkraine  https://t.co/00ejSlszWh https://t.co/NxAGGcUNWj

Happy #PresidentsDay!  Observed the third Monday in February, this holiday recognizes George Washington's birthday and honors the history of the U.S. presidency. @shareamerica provides more information and shows how Americans remember their presidents. https://t.co/jkY1ucq9o5 https://t.co/5geVNinwMG

As we approach one year since Russia’s illegal invasion of Ukraine, @POTUS traveled to Kyiv to express solidarity and support on behalf of the American people. The United States stands strong with Ukraine. https://t.co/6gQNVljSoe

We are grateful to the Government of Türkiye for facilitating the flow of aid into northwest Syria, and for supporting efforts at the @un to authorize multiple crossing points for UN assistance between Türkiye and Syria. https://t.co/fjD4FddIDA https://t.co/phpAHck8p3

The daily lives of Ukrainians changed dramatically on February 24, 2022. And yet, one year later, Ukrainians continue to stand tall – undefeated and unbowed. This is what resiliency looks like. #UnitedWithUkraine https://t.co/V1GWgzJ5VX https://t.co/NxhMd85cmq

Within hours of the first earthquake, the United States quickly mobilized federal agencies and partners to urgently provide life-saving assistance in close coordination with our @nato Ally Türkiye and partner organizations in Syria. More on our efforts: https://t.co/fjD4Fdegt8 https://t.co/bnn6Om2MD8

The U.S. and Türkiye stand in solidarity with the people of Ukraine as they defend their sovereignty and territorial integrity against Russia’s brutal war. The United States welcomes Türkiye's consistent and clear support to stand #UnitedWithUkraine. https://t.co/AuZyuPsGst https://t.co/l5TBTN87F4

The United States has a proud history of working together with Türkiye in times of crisis and on regional security and prosperity. We remain committed to providing assistance to those affected by the devastating earthquakes on February 6. https://t.co/AuZyuPs8CV https://t.co/xxdbHVR2dE
Long-standing U.S. partners continue to respond to the earthquake in Syria and deliver aid to the Syrian people. Learn how they are saving lives and supporting survivors: https://t.co/6PwtqmMlj6

On February 24, 2022, Ukrainians awoke to the nightmare of Russia’s further illegal invasion into their country. These photos, taken before and after that date, demonstrate the resiliency and spirit of Ukraine. #UnitedWithUkraine https://t.co/V1GWgzJDLv https://t.co/YxJqSNgywb

Here at the State Department, cultural diplomacy has long been a pillar of our work.  We run programs to harness the power of art, music, sports, education, and – of course – cuisine, and that helps us connect with other communities and other countries. https://t.co/G9nNY5FzUl

In “The Week at State,” @SecBlinken heads to @MunSecConf; the U.S. continues to support earthquake relief efforts in Türkiye & Syria, @StateDeptSpox unveils the latest @ObserveConflict report, and @DeputySecState speaks to @BrookingsChina. For the latest: https://t.co/58Uc9lIgDz. https://t.co/AlxTBBj8J0

Live now! @SecBlinken participates in a moderated panel discussion on “Visions for Ukraine” with German Foreign Minister @ABaerbock and Ukrainian Foreign Minister @DmytroKuleba in Munich, Germany. Also streaming on YouTube at: https://t.co/PjX4Gdj9Fo https://t.co/PlERpoU8Mw

The resilience of the Ukrainian people has inspired the world. Read a few stories of Ukrainians, young and old, doing their part to protect their country and help the soldiers defending them on the front lines.  https://t.co/vP3dwNlUbh

From New York to Texas and from Ohio to California, people across the U.S. are donating blankets, winter clothes, baby formula, tents, and other supplies to help earthquake survivors in Türkiye and Syria. Learn more about how people are lending support:
https://t.co/KFnEn8WaSn

People across the United States are donating supplies and raising money to assist earthquake survivors in Türkiye and Syria. Learn five ways regular Americans are stepping up to contribute to Türkiye and Syria’s recovery. https://t.co/O3CAls8gEo

In celebration of Black History Month, @ECAatState is recognizing the exemplary contributions of Black Americans to U.S. public diplomacy efforts. We continue to celebrate the lives and achievements of Black Americans who fuel change through their work. https://t.co/pRcd9t0SB9 https://t.co/BMy5Ha9Pfr

The U.S. is helping the Syrian people following the Feb. 6 earthquake, providing assistance through partners including the White Helmets, the SRTFund and @UN agencies. We also announced $85 million for earthquake relief in Türkiye and Syria. Read more: https://t.co/6PwtqmLNty
.@StateDeputySpox on the U.S. Political Declaration at the Summit on Responsible Artificial Intelligence in the Military Domain. #REAIM2023 https://t.co/ytRY3U5ZCE
Live now! @StateDeputySpox briefs reporters from the State Department, also streaming on https://t.co/S9mgUFZTlH. https://t.co/AUTifezhcY
We continue to stand in solidarity with the people of Türkiye and Syria following the devastating earthquake. https://t.co/B6IgwdfQrA

From February 20-22, @POTUS will travel to Poland. He will meet with President Andrzej Duda of Poland to discuss our bilateral cooperation as well as our collective efforts to support Ukraine and bolster @NATO's deterrence. More: https://t.co/4eNBQlk0ki

.@POTUS: @NATO has proved the indispensable Alliance — committed to Europe whole, free, and at peace. Today, we see all too clearly how NATO remains an indispensable Alliance for the world of today and the world of tomorrow. https://t.co/OxYRRP8k7z

As we approach the one-year mark of Russia’s full-scale war on Ukraine, we commend the brave Ukrainians who are fighting for their culture and their nation. The people of Ukraine are not giving up – nor will we. We stand #UnitedWithUkraine. https://t.co/3BR1VyspkS

We continue to stand in solidarity with the people of Türkiye and Syria following the devastating earthquake. 
 https://t.co/B6IgwdfQrA

The U.S. announced $85 million in urgent humanitarian assistance in response to one of the worst earthquakes to hit the region in nearly a century. This new funding is supporting @USAID's partners to deliver urgently needed aid for millions of people in Türkiye and Syria. https://t.co/6wRcbxJyBJ

.@StateDeptSpox on recent arrests in Tunisia of political figures, business leaders, and journalists: We respect the aspirations of the Tunisian people for an independent and transparent judiciary that is able to protect fundamental freedoms for all. https://t.co/r4om0e5kB6

Live now! @StateDeptSpox briefs reporters from the State Department, also streaming on https://t.co/MClOShHYop.
 https://t.co/41jgZ84U6i

.@DeputySecState delivers remarks and participates in a moderated conversation on the U.S. approach to the People’s Republic of China at 11:30 a.m. EST at the Brookings Institution in Washington, D.C. Watch here: https://t.co/Vpj2LkMpaj

The United States is committed to facilitating disaster relief after the deadly February 6 earthquakes. Watch @usairforce personnel unloading a chartered plane containing a 52-bed emergency field hospital tent in Türkiye: https://t.co/ELVQqkQ77G

.@StateDeptSpox on the latest @ObserveConflict report: Russia’s system of forced relocation, re-education, and adoption of Ukraine’s children is a key element of the Kremlin’s systematic efforts to deny and suppress Ukraine’s identity, it’s history, and it’s culture. https://t.co/LPokTVO2ew
Live now! @StateDeptSpox briefs reporters from the State Department, also streaming on https://t.co/Oxy3fW9oxq. https://t.co/YmoxcwXxXx

A new #ConflictObservatory report identifies 43 facilities where Russia has forcibly relocated thousands of Ukraine’s children. Many are now in Russia’s Far East, a continent away from their homes in Ukraine. The children must be returned. @ObserveConflict https://t.co/VksRFZVa52

Happy #ValentinesDay! We are sending #GovLove to all of our followers, friends, and colleagues around the world helping to drive diplomacy forward, one tweet at a time. Wishing you all hope, love, and friendship. https://t.co/kdOIHCraYd

Along with support from local communities and U.S. government assistance, the American business community has also stepped up to help victims of the earthquake in Türkiye and Syria: https://t.co/9iG5l4PBmf

.@State_GCJ to @amanpour on holding Russia accountable for war crimes in 🇺🇦: “There are now three pathways to justice.”
1️⃣ The courts in Ukraine, w/ 65,000+ instances of war crimes
2️⃣ @IntlCrimCourt &
3️⃣ European courts bringing charges against perpetrators in their jurisdictions

.@DeputySecState on the earthquake response in Turkiye and Syria: The U.S. has joined our friends from the ROK, Japan & around the world in sending search & rescue teams, specialized equipment, air support, tents, sanitation facilities, & anything we can to help. https://t.co/pYkO5hm5TH

.@USAID’s Disaster Assistance Response Team search and rescue assisted Turkish rescuers in providing medical consultation to a mother and child freed from wreckage in Adiyaman, nearly five days after the earthquakes. The pair are now receiving care at a medical facility.

Congratulations to Blue Dot Initiative @MDMercyMedia and to @CDMigrante, recipients of the Presidential Award for Extraordinary Efforts to Combat Trafficking in Persons. Their efforts to #EndHumanTrafficking should inspire us all to take action.  @POTUS @JTIP_State https://t.co/NRelp30apC

.@StateDeptSpox on today’s meeting of the President’s Interagency Task Force to Monitor and Combat Trafficking in Persons, chaired by @SecBlinken, where he also presented the Presidential Award for Extraordinary Efforts to Combat Trafficking in Persons to two recipients. https://t.co/8aZewmuJ23

.@StateDeptSpox on our deep concern over Cambodia’s decision to revoke @VODKhmer’s media license: A free and independent press plays a critical role in functioning democracies, providing the public and decision makers with facts, and holding governments to account. https://t.co/gkPf2TebQ5
Live now! @StateDeptSpox briefs reporters from the State Department, also streaming on https://t.co/3fC74iF6xm https://t.co/x2Q7buH3sv
Live now! @SecBlinken chairs the Annual Meeting of the President’s Interagency Task Force to Monitor and Combat Trafficking in Persons at the White House. Watch on YouTube at: https://t.co/sHGIfvuse6

Today at approximately 2 p.m. EST, @DeputySecState hosts a joint press availability with Japanese Vice Minister Mori Takeo and Republic of Korea First Vice Minister Cho Hyundong at the State Department. Watch on Youtube at https://t.co/ETrZGMneI7.

Tune in today at 2 p.m. EST as @SecBlinken chairs the President’s Interagency Task Force to Monitor and Combat Trafficking in Persons. More on our work to #EndHumanTrafficking: https://t.co/sxRlTnNqT4 #PITF @JTIP_State

.@POTUS on PRC: I am committed to work with China where it can advance American interests and benefit the world. But make no mistake: as we made clear last week, if China threatens our sovereignty, we will act to protect our country. And we did. https://t.co/09YItlpzLp

During #SBLVII, the sports community took a moment to pause and recognize the devastation and loss of life in Turkiye and Syria caused by the magnitude 7.8 earthquake.  Learn how you can help at: https://t.co/RZUJRWF0ZZ

The #SuperBowl is a part of American culture that showcases athleticism and teamwork and inspires us all. Which is why we are teaming up with @nfl to spread the inspiration worldwide by supporting youth and women through sports. #SuperBowlLVII https://t.co/zu8ifFrZ5g

.@SecBlinken: President Putin’s war continues to be a strategic failure. He’s failed to overthrow the democratically elected government of Ukraine, to subsume Ukraine into Russia, or to break the will of its people. He’s lost the battles for Kyiv, for Kharkiv, for Kherson. https://t.co/bptz7ev6rp

The United States is committed to supporting Syria and Turkiye in the aftermath of the devastating earthquake. We've already got help on the ground and helicopters supporting in hard-to-access areas. We are coordinating relief efforts with partners across the world. https://t.co/lL7O5n5S0D

Looking for ways to help the people of Türkiye and Syria following the devastating earthquake? Many humanitarian organizations are providing urgent assistance. Here are some ways you can help from wherever you are: https://t.co/RZUJRWF0ZZ

In the “Week at State,” @SecBlinken addresses the initial U.S. earthquake response to Turkiye and Syria, convenes the fourth and final COVID-19 GAP Ministerial, and launches the #DiplomaticCulinaryPartnership with the @BeardFoundation. For more visit https://t.co/58Uc9lIgDz. https://t.co/1qFcVNHNDi

We’re excited about the #DiplomaticCulinaryPartnership with @us_protocol and the @beardfoundation. Hear from @secblinken and @chefjoseandres and see a few of the amazing chefs that will be part of this partnership. They know that diplomacy begins with great food. https://t.co/8OsobUzRWI

The United States is excited to serve as @apec host in 2023. During #USAPEC2023 we will prioritize building an interconnected and resilient Asia-Pacific region, enabling an innovative environment, and affirming an inclusive and equitable future for all. https://t.co/lPdsHM90iU https://t.co/uEQ1Bs1HYd

.@SecBlinken at the final #COVIDMinisterial: We know that a pandemic is not just a health crisis – it’s a security crisis; it’s an economic crisis; it’s a humanitarian crisis.  Health security is national security. https://t.co/X0Qo5uOEwS

The United States is the largest investor in Brazil, including in many of their most innovative companies. We will continue to look for opportunities with @LulaOficial and Brazil to strengthen our economic ties and increase our large and growing trade and investment relationship. https://t.co/Zr3fIsnMqp

In today’s briefing @StateDeputySpox provided an overview of Deputy Assistant Secretary Lord’s travel to Sudan, highlighted U.S. actions against corrupt actors in Bulgaria, and condemned Russia’s latest attacks on Ukraine’s infrastructure. Read more: https://t.co/mJzkS5KxEy

The U.S. is Brazil’s second-largest export market. Further, the United States and Brazil share an economic relationship valued at more than $100 billion, creating thousands of jobs and promoting prosperity in both countries. https://t.co/Uyjhx1mSge

The United States is committed to supporting Syria and Turkiye in the aftermath of the devastating earthquake. We've already got help on the ground and helicopters supporting in hard-to-access areas. We are coordinating relief efforts with partners across the world. https://t.co/t6lYIEsxXH
